questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes India's foreign trade structure at the time of independence?
questions.option_3: Export of raw materials and import of manufactured goods

questions.question: Employment patterns during 1947-1991 showed limited diversification. Which sector employed the largest share of India’s workforce throughout this period?
Which of the following indicators is NOT included in the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was established to formalise inflation targeting. How many members constitute India’s MPC?
Human development also involves freedom, security and dignity in life. The first Human Development Report was published in which year?
Which institution played a major role in supporting India’s liberalisation policy during the 1991 crisis?
What was one of the major characteristics of India’s economy at the time of independence in 1947?
questions.option_2: Heavy dependence on agriculture and low productivity

questions.question: What is the key reason governments promote Public-Private Partnerships in infrastructure development?
questions.option_2: To improve efficiency and innovation through private sector expertise
questions.option_3: Due to lack of trained manpower
questions.option_4: To address fiscal constraints and reduce liabilities

questions.question: In the post-reform era, fiscal prudence became central to macroeconomic stability. Which of the following Acts was enacted in 2003 to institutionalise fiscal discipline in India?
questions.option_1: Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act

questions.question: In which of the following years was the first ever National Agriculture Policy announced?
What has been the trend in India’s per capita income since the 1991 reforms?
Which sector in India saw significant growth due to globalisation and outsourcing after the 1990s?
Liberalisation reduced government control over economic activities. What did liberalisation in 1991 primarily aim to eliminate?
Where was rural development particularly emphasised in the Seventh Five-Year Plan?
Which of the following sectors includes banking, communication and transport?
The Planning Commission was the nodal body for central planning in India. Who served as the first Chairman of the Planning Commission?
What is the primary function of infrastructure in an economy?
Which of the following best defines globalisation in the context of post 1991 economic reforms in India?
questions.option_1: Integration of Indian economy with the global economy

questions.question: The Green Revolution in India primarily led to an increase in the production of which crops?
Which Five Year Plan marked the beginning of the Green Revolution in India?
Developing countries have often experienced mixed outcomes from globalisation. Which of the following is a potential disadvantage of globalisation for developing economies?
Which of the following industries was operational in India at the time of independence?
How did globalisation affect India’s economic growth?
What was the reason for stagnation in the agricultural sector during the colonial rule?
questions.option_1: Establishment of large scale industries by the colonial government
questions.option_2: Extreme commercialisation of agriculture by the colonial government
questions.option_3: Imposition of high taxes on the food grains and cash crops by the colonial government
questions.option_4: Various systems of land settlement that were introduced by the colonial government

questions.question: What percentage of the Indian population was dependent on agriculture around the time of independence?
